body.title: CLOSED: Refactor goal_continuation.py into GoalContinuation(IgorBase)
body.result: GoalContinuation(IgorBase) refactor: 2KB bash cap, self.log, forensic_logger replaced; tests updated
body.description:
goal_continuation.py uses standalone functions and logging.getLogger() — the same pattern pe_chain had before T-pe-chain-igorbase. The _run_bash() helper truncates output to 500 chars and logs only via Python stdlib logging; claim/show failures are silently truncated.

Refactor into GoalContinuation(IgorBase):
- run_goal_continuation() and _run_bash(), _extract_ticket_id(), etc. become methods
- Module-level run_goal_continuation(**_) stays as a thin wrapper for registry compatibility (the Tool registration at the bottom of the file must continue to work)
- _run_bash() output logged via self.log.info('BASH cmd=%s out=%s', cmd, out[:2000]) — 2KB cap (was 500 chars), logged to standard log not lost
- Replace logging.getLogger(__name__) with self.log throughout
- Replace forensic_logger lazy imports (there are ~2 in this file) with self.log.error
- Update module docstring: add GoalContinuation class to the overview section

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/goal_continuation.py
**Design rules:** OOP-first (class required), docs-live-in-code (module docstring must mention GoalContinuation class)
**Scope boundary:** goal_continuation.py only. The module-level run_goal_continuation(**_) wrapper keeps the Tool registry hook working unchanged — the registered fn must still be callable with no args.
**Test plan:** Existing goal_continuation behavior unchanged. Add one test: mock _run_bash (or the subprocess call) to return a 600-char string and verify the logged output is NOT truncated to 500 chars. Run full test suite — green required.
